chapter_goal: Make large-sample inference visible as local geometry: shrinking estimator clouds, curved-family projection, ancillary foliations, first-order efficiency, second-order curvature terms, and hypothesis-test boundaries.

source_span_read.used_for: orientation on section order, terminology, formulas, theorem roles, and concept boundaries only

coverage_refresh: estimation error, bias, consistency, Cramer-Rao bound, MLE first-order efficiency
exponential-family observed point and expectation-coordinate MLE
curved exponential family as a submanifold of an ambient exponential family
ancillary fibers, local (u, v) coordinates, and orthogonality as first-order efficiency
normalized errors, Jacobian blocks, and Schur-complement first-order covariance
bias correction and second-order covariance contributions from model, coordinate, and ancillary curvature
hypothesis-test rejection boundaries as N-dependent ancillary hypersurfaces
chapter remarks on Bayes estimators, third-order MLE limits, local exponential families, singular models, and non-regular Finsler-like behavior

visual_sequence.inspection_target: sample clouds contract at the inverse Fisher rate
ambient observed point projects back to curved S
visual_sequence.validation: projection residual is nearly orthogonal to tangent

visual_sequence.inspection_target: orthogonal and tilted fibers encode different estimators
first-order variance penalty as fibers tilt away from orthogonality
visual_sequence.validation: orthogonal fiber reaches the Cramer-Rao factor

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Section 7.4 covariance from normalized errors in (u, v) coordinates
visual_sequence.validation: Schur-complement covariance matches inverse metric and simulation
same curve and fiber geometry as static plots

visual_sequence.inspection_target: where the curved model departs from its tangent approximation
visual_sequence.validation: curvature is nonnegative and sampled along S
terms are nonnegative and MLE ancillary term is zero in the toy decomposition

visual_sequence.inspection_target: orthogonal and tilted estimators under simulation
visual_sequence.validation: variance decreases with N and tilted estimator remains worse

visual_sequence.inspection_target: testing boundaries approach the null and behave like ancillary hypersurfaces
visual_sequence.validation: boundary distances shrink as N grows

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Rao, Wald, likelihood-ratio, and locally most powerful tests share first-order geometry but differ beyond it
visual_sequence.validation: named-test table includes all four source-named tests

library_routing.why: closed-form covariance makes the geometry inspectable
a planar embedded curve exposes tangent, normal, and residual checks
the local metric block calculation is small enough to compute directly
3D rotation makes the (u, v) foliation easier to inspect
the theorem is a nonnegative contribution decomposition, naturally shown as a table and stacked bars
